# LLM Integration Checklist: TACD-002 Capability Discovery Directive and Agent Updates

**Purpose**: Validate requirement quality for capability-first LLM agent guidance, shared directive binding, evidence reporting, metadata preservation, and scope boundaries.
**Created**: 2026-06-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are capability-first selection requirements defined for all TACD-002-scoped Claude agents, Codex agents, shared references, and generated payload copies? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-004, Contract §Scope]
- [ ] CHK002 Are all required capability categories enumerated and aligned between the spec, data model, and contract? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, Data Model §Capability Discovery Directive, Contract §Capability Selection]
- [x] CHK003 Does the spec define that selection must be task-need driven rather than a renamed fixed vendor or capability fallback chain? [Resolved, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-005, Contract §Capability Selection]
- [x] CHK004 Does the spec define selection criteria for multiple installed capabilities that can satisfy the same need? [Resolved, Spec §FR-005, Data Model §Capability Discovery Directive, Contract §Capability Selection]
- [ ] CHK005 Are no-optional-capability fallback requirements complete for local files, native platform context, and repo-local helpers? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Story 2 AC-1]
- [ ] CHK006 Are TACD-003 prerequisite/user-facing messaging and TACD-004 deterministic/eval enforcement exclusions explicit across requirements and success criteria? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-014, Spec §SC-006]

## Directive Binding And Runtime Parity

- [ ] CHK007 Is the shared directive path specified exactly and consistently across clarifications, functional requirements, plan, and contract? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-003, Plan §Summary, Contract §Directive Binding]
- [ ] CHK008 Are acceptable runtime-specific equivalents defined for Codex installed TOML agents without changing the semantic behavior contract? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004, Contract §Directive Binding]
- [x] CHK009 Is the source-note marker for compact Codex TOML equivalents specified precisely enough for reviewer evidence before TACD-004 enforcement exists? [Resolved, Spec §FR-004, Contract §Directive Binding, Quickstart §Validation Scenario 1]
- [ ] CHK010 Are generated Codex skill payload path rewrites treated as runtime-specific pointer mechanics rather than semantic changes? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Research §Runtime-Specific Pointers]
- [ ] CHK011 Can Claude and Codex reviewers independently trace every scoped guidance surface to either the shared directive or an approved equivalent? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002, Plan §Declared File Operations]

## Evidence Reporting Quality

- [ ] CHK012 Is the compact evidence wording specified exactly with capability path, citations or local files, confidence, and rationale? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007, Contract §Required Evidence Output]
- [ ] CHK013 Are fallback evidence disclosures specified for missing or unusable installed capabilities with lowered confidence? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Contract §Required Evidence Output]
- [ ] CHK014 Are inventory-disclosure limits defined so normal answers report selected capability path only, while troubleshooting and PR evidence can include full inventories? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Contract §Inventory Disclosure]
- [ ] CHK015 Are evidence-reporting requirements traceable to PR packet expectations and generated payload evidence? [Traceability, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements, Contract §Generated Payload Refresh]

## Metadata And Formerly Named Tools

- [ ] CHK016 Are formerly named optional tools preserved only as ordinary installed capabilities selected through discovery, metadata, historical references, or provenance? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-011]
- [ ] CHK017 Are schema-required metadata IDs separated from active behavior surfaces in the data model and PR packet requirements? [Clarity, Data Model §Schema Metadata ID, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements]
- [ ] CHK018 Are behavior surfaces clearly identified as agent body text, Codex TOML developer instructions, and shared reference prose? [Completeness, Contract §Metadata Preservation]
- [ ] CHK019 Are preserved-ID review requirements measurable with file, field, classification, and behavior-scan result? [Measurability, Spec §FR-010, Contract §Metadata Preservation]

## Generated Payload And Source-Of-Truth Alignment

- [ ] CHK020 Does the spec require generated `dist/**` payloads to be refreshed from source rather than hand-edited? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012, Plan §Declared File Operations]
- [ ] CHK021 Are required payload refresh evidence elements complete: builder invocation, payload roots, paired diffs, default suite, and second rebuild idempotence? [Completeness, Contract §Generated Payload Refresh, Quickstart §Validation Scenario 4]
- [ ] CHK022 Are source-of-truth and generated payload evidence separated in a way that supports reviewability expectations? [Consistency, Plan §Structure Decision, Preset speckit-pro-reviewability]

## Scenario Coverage And Scope Boundaries

- [ ] CHK023 Are primary, fallback, parity, generated-payload, metadata, and scope-guard scenarios covered by independent tests or validation scenarios? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios, Quickstart §Validation Scenarios]
- [ ] CHK024 Are edge cases for stale payloads, historical references, metadata IDs, and runtime pointer resolution included? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK025 Are TACD-003 and TACD-004 deferred-work boundaries preserved without weakening TACD-002 acceptance criteria? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-014, Research §Deferred Enforcement]

## Notes

- Generated from prompt focus: capability-first discovery semantics, directive pointer coverage, evidence reporting, formerly named tool preservation, and avoiding a renamed fixed vendor fallback chain.
